description:
Replace VMware-specific modules with hypervisor-abstracted equivalents.

## Changes

### 1. `nixos-modules/devbox-guest.nix` (new, replaces vmware-fusion.nix)
Hypervisor-aware guest configuration:
- `utm`/`kvm`: QEMU guest agent, virtio drivers, spice-vdagent (if interactive)
- `vmware`: existing open-vm-tools config (preserve current behavior)

```nix
lib.mkIf machine.features.devbox {
  # Common devbox guest config
  boot.initrd.availableKernelModules = lib.optionals (machine.devbox.hypervisor != "vmware") [
    "virtio_pci" "virtio_blk" "virtio_net" "virtio_scsi" "9p" "9pnet_virtio"
  ] ++ lib.optionals (machine.devbox.hypervisor == "vmware") [
    "nvme"
  ];

  # Hypervisor-specific guest tools
  virtualisation.vmware.guest = lib.mkIf (machine.devbox.hypervisor == "vmware") { ... };
  services.qemuGuest = lib.mkIf (machine.devbox.hypervisor != "vmware") { ... };
  services.spice-vdagentd = lib.mkIf (machine.features.interactive && machine.devbox.hypervisor != "vmware") { ... };
}
```

### 2. Update `nixos-modules/default.nix`
- Remove imports of `vmware-fusion.nix` and `vmware-image.nix`
- Add imports of `devbox-guest.nix` and `devbox-image.nix`

### 3. Deprecation
- Keep `fusion` feature working (mapped from `devbox.hypervisor = "vmware"`)
- Mark vmware-fusion.nix and vmware-image.nix for removal after migration

## Acceptance
- Existing VMware devboxes still build and function
- New UTM/KVM devboxes get correct guest tooling
- `vmware-fusion.nix` and `vmware-image.nix` are no longer imported directly
title: Migrate fusion feature to devbox system
